subject: procure.blog TLDR: Apr 10
preheader: 6 AI procurement stories you need to know

items.title: US 2026 National AI Policy Framework proposes sweeping federal procurement standards
items.summary: The White House released a national AI policy framework that would preempt state-level AI rules and require all federal procurement to follow standardized unbiased-AI principles. Federal contractors face new compliance requirements that could reshape how AI vendors sell to government agencies.

items.title: GSA's New AI Clause Would Mandate U.S.-Made AI Systems in Procurement Contracts
items.summary: A proposed GSA clause would require all contractors and subcontractors to use American-made AI systems for federal work, with broad data rights and reporting obligations attached.

items.title: NIST to Address Standards for AI in Agentic Procurement
items.summary: NIST announced plans for a public-private convening in April 2026 to set standards for AI agents used in commercial procurement, following a surge in autonomous purchasing tools.

items.title: New research urges procurement teams to redefine supplier risk management in 2026
items.summary: Studies from Achilles and the World Economic Forum call for expanded AI-based supplier risk approaches to handle volatility, compliance gaps, and data quality issues.

items.title: Ironclad Unveils AI Assistant and Agents for Contract Intelligence Platform
items.summary: 50% reduction in contract intake time reported by early adopters. Ironclad's new AI agents turn static contracts into real-time operational intelligence with automated extraction and alerts.

items.title: Why 2026 Chip Shortages Persist and How Procurement Tech Startups Are Responding
items.summary: AI-driven supplier analytics and real-time ECAD integrations are helping procurement teams navigate ongoing semiconductor shortages.
